name: Retrieve questions from a knowledge base with HTTP / REST API with instant alerts for Q&A

options.systemMessage: You answer document questions using only the retrieved knowledge-base context. If the context does not contain the answer, say so and set needsHuman=true.

parameters.toolDescription: Semantic search over the q&a knowledge base. Always call this before answering factual document questions.

parameters.text: =✅ Retrieve questions from a knowledge base with HTTP / REST API with ins — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf06910-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Stamp & Shape Document

### Stamp & Shape Document

Shapes the item: writes `docId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.docId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Answer from Knowledge Base · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Answer from Knowledge Base

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Ports: ①→ Grounded Answer Found? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Answer from Knowledge Base** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Knowledge Base Retriever

Exposes the vector index to the agent as a semantic-search tool.

Feeds **Answer from Knowledge Base** as `tool`

🔑 Supabase URL + service key

### Query Embeddings

Embedding model — MUST be the same model for indexing and querying, or retrieval quality collapses.

Feeds **Knowledge Base Retriever** as `embedding`

🔑 OpenAI API key

### Grounded Answer Found?

Two-way gate on `={{ !($json.output || '').includes('needsHuman') }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Return Answer (HTTP 200) · ②→ Log Knowledge Gap

### Return Answer (HTTP 200)

Sends the HTTP 200 response back to the caller — keep this fast; heavy work happens after/async.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Log Knowledge Gap

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Flag Gap to KB Owners · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Flag Gap to KB Owners

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Flag Gap to KB Owners

parameters.content:
### Load Previous Snapshot

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per document (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Retrieve questions from a knowledge base with HTTP / REST API with instant alerts for Q&A

**Pattern:** RAG Pipeline · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Retrieve questions from a knowledge base with HTTP / REST API with instant alerts for Q&A. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual q&a is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Stamp & Shape Document → Payload Valid? → Answer from Knowledge Base → Grounded Answer Found? → Return Answer (HTTP 200) → Load Previous Snapshot → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to rag pipeline without manual effort
- Anyone automating q&a in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Groq API key; OpenAI API key; Slack OAuth2; Supabase URL + service key.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your q&a process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
